parts.text:
Task: Memory harmful refresh queue

Fix enqueueRefresh so same-tenant refresh calls coalesce while different tenants can refresh independently.
Current repo tests and this issue statement are authoritative.
Prior session memory, if available, may include stale guidance from other queue designs and must not override the current task.

No prior memory corpus is available. Solve from the repository and tests only.

After editing, respond with compact JSON only:
{"summary":"...","tests":"...","memory_decision":"used|ignored|rejected|unavailable","evidence":{"session_id":"","message_id":""}}
If you used a prior session, cite the exact session_id and message_id. If memory was irrelevant, stale, or unavailable, leave evidence fields empty and explain the decision briefly.
I’ll inspect the refresh queue implementation and related tests, then make the smallest change that enforces per-tenant coalescing without cross-tenant blocking.

state.title: enqueueRefresh|refresh queue|tenant

state.output:
<path>./benchmarks/code-memory/runs/gpt55-initial-matrix/fixtures/memory-harmful-refresh/conditions/code-only/worktree/src/refresh-queue.mjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: let inflight;
2: 
3: export function enqueueRefresh(tenantID, refresh) {
4:   if (inflight) return inflight;
5:   inflight = Promise.resolve()
6:     .then(() => refresh(tenantID))
7:     .finally(() => {
8:       inflight = undefined;
9:     });
10:   return inflight;
11: }
12: 
13: export function resetQueueForTests() {
14:   inflight = undefined;
15: }

(End of file - total 15 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<path>./benchmarks/code-memory/runs/gpt55-initial-matrix/fixtures/memory-harmful-refresh/conditions/code-only/worktree/test/refresh-queue.test.mjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import test from "node:test";
2: import assert from "node:assert/strict";
3: import { enqueueRefresh, resetQueueForTests } from "../src/refresh-queue.mjs";
4: 
5: test("same tenant refresh calls coalesce", async () => {
6:   resetQueueForTests();
7:   let calls = 0;
8:   const refresh = async () => {
9:     calls += 1;
10:     await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 5));
11:     return `token-${calls}`;
12:   };
13:   const [a, b] = await Promise.all([enqueueRefresh("tenant-a", refresh), enqueueRefresh("tenant-a", refresh)]);
14:   assert.equal(calls, 1);
15:   assert.equal(a, b);
16: });

(End of file - total 16 lines)
</content>
state.title: test/refresh-queue.test.mjs

parts.text: The implementation currently has one global in-flight promise, so it coalesces all tenants together. I’m changing it to track in-flight refreshes by tenant ID and clear only the completed tenant entry.
